title: An Extraordinary Account of a Dangerous Marriage
description: Gisèle Pelicot’s memoir is an astonishingly honest look at her life with a man who did the unthinkable.
hook: Gisèle Pelicot's unflinching memoir explores complicity, trauma, and selfhood within an extraordinary marriage gone catastrophically wrong.

title: Looking at Attention
description: “We ‘are’ our attention; but when we are attentive, we aren’t ‘there’—at least not for others. And perhaps not even for ourselves.”
hook: A philosophical meditation on attention itself—how consciousness shapes reality and the paradox of self-awareness in witnessing.

title: Georgi Gospodinov: ‘Jorge Luis Borges gave me an exhilarating sense of freedom’
description:
The Bulgarian Booker winner on the letter he wrote to JD Salinger, the allure of Homer’s Odyssey and the magic of Thomas Mann

My earliest reading memory
I was taught to read quite early, at five or six, probably so that I would sit quietly and not be a nuisance to the adults. And it worked. Once I’d entered a book, I didn’t want to come out. I remember how Hans Christian Andersen’s The Little Match Girl turned my heart upside down. I was living with my grandmothe
hook: Booker-winning author Gospodinov traces literary lineage from Borges to Mann, revealing how modernist writers shaped his creative freedom.

title: Issey Miyake
description:
‘The only thing I retained from Grose’s lecture about Lacanian psychoanalysis was a comment about clothes.’ 

Akshi Singh on clothes, fantasy and Issey Miyake.

The post Issey Miyake appeared first on Granta.
hook: Akshi Singh explores fashion as Lacanian psychoanalysis—how Issey Miyake's designs mediate desire, fantasy, and the clothed body.

title: The Moment review – it's not that easy being green
description: Charli XCX stars as a fictionalised version of herself in Aidan Zamiri's debut, telling the mostly, sort of, kind of true story of 2024's brat summer.
hook: Charli XCX plays herself in a semi-fictionalized account of 2024's 'brat summer'—high/low cultural moment crystallized.

title: Facing a mental health crisis, an NJ school pulled a beloved novel from English class
description: Junot Díaz's The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao was removed from an English class at the public school. PEN America says it's part of a trend of scrubbing literature dealing with uncomfortable topics.
hook: A school pulls Junot Díaz's acclaimed novel citing mental health concerns—exposing the culture war's real impact on literary education.

title: A Novel That Probes Parasocial Relationships Between Fan and Artist
description:
The first thing Jenny Tinghui Zhang and I bonded over when we met at the Sewanee Writers’ Conference, aside from both being writers, was that we were both devoted Lorde fans. Every time I meet someone who enjoys her music, it’s almost guaranteed that we immediately have a great friendship. A couple hours later, Zhang […]

The post A Novel That Probes Parasocial Relationshi
hook: Jenny Tinghui Zhang's novel investigates parasocial fandom and devotion—the interior psychology of artist-obsessed modern life.
